title: Small Matters
text: Audio is no small matter but a cork pop sound effect may seem small. The critique concerned the quality of a sound effect because it registered out of place with the other audio components. Sound engineering is a complex field of study. Turning instead to practical application, Foley sound effects for movies are considered successful if they are not heard (Talijan, 2024).

That statement interpreted as sound as part of the experience led to the sound effect being elongated, for definition, and tempered, for layering, so it isn't a focal point. Seamlessness also matters.

title: Interpretation Matters
text: The flat design used for a motion video of dancers didn't read clearly. Symbols should direct the viewer to content or form (Lawes, 2023). The symbols of people were instead leading to questions and distractions.

They were clarified by bending the arms thereby removing their zombified characterization. Just as in language where some words have undocumented meaning, so it is with symbols and icons. The brand is not planning on eating anybody, promise.

title: Emotions Matter
text: A product designer consulted for the Colony Of Voice vision board came back with, '...the sea in the background creates a slightly unsettled feeling, almost a bit seasick.' The vision board is a communication tool for the design work, imagery can be used to communicate atmosphere (Felton, 2013), and a rocky ship is not a great foundational atmosphere for a brand.
Wrong placement of the theme of chaos turned sour so the imagery was removed. Careful elimination can enhance the message and improve clarity. The essentials will surface as the removal is conducted.

title: Purpose Matters
text: There shouldn't be elements that do not contribute to the design. Flourishes or decorations that have no purpose and add no relevant information to the design solution. Thereby they can damage the design results (Karjaluoto, 2014).

So the feedback about a curved line looking like a 'spine', even though curved lines were part of the look and feel outline, meant the element had to be removed. The gaps between intentions and reception sometimes reside in nuance.

title: Context Matters
text: Templates are valuable for consistency especially for repetitive tasks. They are a poke-yoke or mistake-proofing mechanisms. However, showing templates to a client can be a less than awe-inspiring opportunity. Based upon feedback asking for adding content, the insight of contextual application surfaced. Instead showing the tool, show the outcome.

Not only did this drive a better presentation of the asset but it also led to the addition of a deliverable, a word document template in addition to the letterhead template because sometimes the practicality of margins printing correctly matter as much as context.

title: Motion Matters
text: The critique of the Colony of Voice animated logo pointed out issues with speed. Velocity in motion design is a carefully managed quality built upon traditional animation techniques, most notably from Disney, and sometimes through the digi cel techniques (frame by frame), contribute to the outcome (Shaw & Colette, 2022).

The number of elements in motion was reduced, and for the main moving logo, one of the 12 principles of animation was applied — squash and stretch. The goal was accomplished in a much cleaner presentation.

title: Polish Matters
text: The style guide's design intentions centered on experiential education instead of referential memorization. The user of the guide would experience what the brand voice and tone is and is not instead of reading an encyclopedia or rules. Elements of the attempt were critiqued for lack of clarity.
Sometimes words matter and polish is in the comprehensiveness. The descriptions were added to avoid poor intuitive interpretations of likely unclear but impressive imagery.

title: Artificial (AI) Matters
text: Artificial intelligence is going to be part of the future design process even as the soul screams dying from anguish. Wait a second, didn't graphic designers used to work with scissors, illustrators with pencils, and videographers in silence? Some forward thinkers believe AI will open up new horizons for designers (Alkahteeb et al., 2025). We can hope there will still be designers.

The self-evaluation of the video explanation identified gaps between wishes and capabilities. Lacking a film crew, a budget, and time, the decision to use AI to turn a picture of a vertical urban pig farm into a short motion video felt less traumatic than expected with oinking sound effects and all. Necessity is the mother of invention for those who don't mind clichés.
